samples.xml_description:
<module id="serial2parallel">
    <description>Serial-Parallel Conversion Circuit</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="din_serial">Serial input data.</input>
        <input id="din_valid">Validity signal for input data.</input>
        <output id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data (8 bits wide).</output>
        <output id="dout_valid">Validity signal for the output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">Idle state.</state>
        <state id="LOAD">Load state.</state>
        <state id="OUTPUT">Output state.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="cnt" width_description="[3:0]">Counter to keep track of the number of serial input data bits received.</reg>
        <reg id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data register.</reg>
        <reg id="dout_valid">Valid signal for the output data.</reg>
        
        <always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)>
            if (~rst_n) begin
                cnt <= 4'd0;
                dout_parallel <= 8'd0;
                dout_valid <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                case (cnt)
                    IDLE: begin
                        if (din_valid) begin
                            dout_parallel[cnt] <= din_serial;
                            cnt <= cnt + 1;
                        end
                    end
                    LOAD: begin
                        if (din_valid) begin
                            dout_parallel[cnt] <= din_serial;
                            cnt <= cnt + 1;
                        end
                    end
                    OUTPUT: begin
                        dout_valid <= 1'b1;
                        cnt <= 4'd0;
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        
        <always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)> // Output logic
            if (~rst_n) begin
                dout_valid <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                if (cnt == 4'd7 && din_valid) begin
                    dout_valid <= 1'b1;
                end else begin
                    dout_valid <= 1'b0;
                end
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>
        Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal.
        If reset is active, initialize the counter, parallel output data, and valid signal to their respective default values.
        Otherwise, based on the current state of the counter, update the parallel output data and increment the counter.
        Output logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal.
        If reset is active, set the valid signal to its default value.
        Otherwise, check if the counter has reached the maximum value and the input data is valid. If so, set the valid signal to 1.
        Otherwise, set the valid signal to 0.
    </implementation>
</module>
